turns.student: What brings you in today?
turns.patient: I've been having some pretty uncomfortable symptoms for the past three days, doc. I get this tightness in my chest that feels like pressure.

turns.student: Can you describe the main symptom you're experiencing?
turns.patient: The chest tightness is a substernal sensation - it's right behind my breastbone and feels like a heavy pressure. It's been consistent for three days now, and I've noticed it gets worse when I exert myself or do anything physical.

turns.student: When did this start?
turns.patient: It started about 3 days ago, just out of the blue. No warning signs or nothing, just woke up one morning and it was there.

turns.student: How often does it happen?
turns.patient: It happens pretty frequently - at least a few times a day, sometimes more often when I'm under stress or experiencing physical exertion. I'd say 5-6 episodes per day, but they can range from mild to pretty severe.

turns.student: How severe is it on a scale of 1 to 10?
turns.patient: It's around an 8 out of 10 when it hits its peak - it's really uncomfortable and makes me feel like I'm having trouble breathing.

turns.student: Do you notice any other symptoms when it happens?
turns.patient: Sometimes I get a little lightheaded and short of breath when it gets bad. And my arm on that side - it feels like there's a tingling sensation in it during the more severe episodes.

turns.student: Does it get worse with any particular activity?
turns.patient: Yeah, it definitely gets worse when I'm doing anything physical. Like, if I'm hauling a heavy load on my truck, it'll feel like it's constricting even more.

turns.student: Do you have any sweating, nausea, or dizziness with it?
turns.patient: I do get some mild nausea during the episodes, but no vomiting.

turns.student: Does the pain or discomfort spread to any other part of your body?
turns.patient: No, it's just my chest and arm - sometimes the tightness in my chest will radiate a bit more than usual, but mostly it stays confined to that area.

turns.student: Do you have any swelling in your legs or feet?
turns.patient: Yes, I've noticed some mild bilateral ankle swelling lately. My shoes feel tighter than usual.

turns.student: What medications are you currently taking?
turns.patient: I'm taking amlodipine 10mg daily for my hypertension, but to be honest, I don't always remember to take it due to my irregular schedule on the road.

turns.student: Do you have any medical conditions like high blood pressure or diabetes?
turns.patient: I've got high blood pressure and type 2 diabetes. My doctor told me to take metformin for my diabetes, so I do that fairly consistently - 1000mg twice a day.

turns.student: Have you ever had your cholesterol checked?
turns.patient: Yeah, I did have it checked about a year ago during my last routine checkup. The doctor told me my cholesterol was high, but I never went back to get it treated.

turns.student: Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
turns.patient: Yeah, I smoke half a pack of cigarettes per day, and on weekends, I usually have 1-2 beers. It's just something I do socially.

turns.student: How would you describe your diet and exercise habits?
turns.patient: My diet is pretty heavy in fast food - I'd say I eat it 5-6 days a week.

turns.student: Has anyone in your family had heart disease or heart attacks?
turns.patient: My father actually passed away from a heart attack when he was 52.

turns.student: Has anyone in your family had other serious medical conditions?
turns.patient: My dad had a heart attack and didn't make it. He was only 52 at the time, which is pretty young.

turns.student: When was the last time you saw a doctor?
turns.patient: I last saw a doctor about 1 year ago for a routine checkup.

turns.student: Do you have any allergies to medications?
turns.patient: I'm actually feeling a bit more anxious now that we're discussing my symptoms and medical history. I just hope we can figure out what's going on and get me feeling better soon.

turns.student: Is there anything else you think I should know about your health?
turns.patient: I've been feeling really stressed out and anxious lately, especially since this chest tightness started.
